page: Amazon Web Services r7a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include discrete GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 588,964.61 and a Passmark CPU score of 143,337.26. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only large block bandwidth of 652.3 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth of 309.09 GB/sec. Single-core performance is strong, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3,075.77. The instance also excels in memory-bound and database operations, achieving 323.08 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 109.85 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7a.48xlarge offers high resource density for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. The combination of 192 dedicated cores and 1.5 TB of DDR5 RAM makes it highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, complex data compilation, and memory-bound scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.7 GHz, the instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 588,964.61 and uncached read bandwidth of 652.3 GB/sec. This high-density memory profile, combined with strong single-core and multi-core processing capabilities, makes the r7a.48xlarge highly efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, built for large-scale in-memory databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.48xlarge features 192 AMD EPYC cores and 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 RAM for demanding in-memory enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU performance
Features 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and data-heavy enterprise applications
